message:
fix(pager): patch page_count in SQLite header on commit to prevent malformed DB

The SQLite file header's page_count field (offset 28..32) was never
updated after new pages were allocated and committed.  The initial
header was written with page_count=1 at database creation, but
subsequent commits only updated the in-memory db_size without
re-serializing the header.  External SQLite readers rely on this
header field, so they would see page_count=1 while the file actually
contained dozens of pages, causing `PRAGMA integrity_check` to fail
with "database disk image is malformed".

The fix patches page 1's header bytes (page_count, change_counter,
and version_valid_for) in three commit paths:
  - commit_journal: after writing dirty pages to the database file
  - commit_wal: when writing page 1 to the WAL
  - checkpoint writer: when transferring page 1 from WAL to DB file

Also updates version_valid_for to match change_counter so SQLite
trusts the header page_count rather than recomputing from file size.

Fixes #8

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
